strengths: Hands-on distributed systems experience at production scale — Sociomantic/Dunnhumby role involved microservices handling millions of HTTP requests per second with real-time, low-latency constraints.
Early-stage agentic AI exposure: current role at re:cinq includes MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks, showing awareness of the agentic paradigm.
Breadth across full-stack, embedded, event-driven, and open-source platform development demonstrates strong technical adaptability.
concerns: No executive or people-leadership experience at any level — the resume shows no evidence of managing, hiring, or growing engineering teams, let alone scaling from 10 to 30+ engineers.
Significant seniority mismatch: the candidate is a Staff/Senior Software Developer applying for a C-suite role, with no VP, Director, or Head of Engineering tenure in their history.

Agentic AI involvement is superficial and very recent (April 2025 onwards) — no demonstrated production deployment of multi-agent systems, LLM evaluation pipelines, or AI observability infrastructure at commercial scale.
response.justification: The candidate has a solid individual-contributor profile with credible distributed systems depth and early agentic tooling experience, but is fundamentally misaligned with the CTO role: there is no evidence of team leadership, organizational scaling, board communication, fintech compliance or credit-system architecture, or production multi-agent AI systems — all of which are hard requirements for this position.
